title: Level2 Trading Strategy
description: Schema for a Level2 automated trading strategy created through the visual no-code strategy builder. Defines the structure of strategy definitions including entry and exit conditions, risk management rules, position configuration, and instrument settings.

userId.description: The identifier of the user who owns this strategy

name.description: A human-readable name for the trading strategy

description.description: A description of the strategy's purpose and trading logic

instrument.description: The primary ticker symbol this strategy trades (e.g., AAPL, MSFT)

instrumentType.description: The type of financial instrument being traded

timeframe.description: The primary chart timeframe the strategy operates on

status.description: The current lifecycle status of the strategy

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the strategy was created

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the strategy was last modified

deployedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the strategy was last deployed for live trading

StrategyDefinition.description: The core strategy definition containing entry conditions, exit conditions, risk management, and position configuration built using the Level2 visual drag-and-drop interface.

entryConditions.description: Ordered list of conditions that must be satisfied to trigger a trade entry

exitConditions.description: Ordered list of conditions that trigger closing an open position

StrategyCondition.description: A single condition in the strategy logic, representing a comparison between a technical indicator or price value and a target value or another indicator.

id.description: Unique identifier for this condition within the strategy

indicator.description: The technical indicator or data source (e.g., SMA, EMA, RSI, MACD, BollingerBands, Price, Volume). Level2 supports 104 technical indicators.

parameters.description: Configuration parameters for the indicator, such as period length, source field, or standard deviation multiplier

operator.description: The comparison operator that defines the relationship between the indicator value and the compare value

compareValue.description: The target value or indicator configuration to compare against. Can be a numeric threshold, a string reference, or a nested indicator object.

logicalOperator.description: Defines how this condition combines with the next condition in the sequence

RiskManagement.description: Risk management settings that control loss limits and profit targets for the strategy.

stopLoss.description: Stop loss value as a percentage or absolute price distance

stopLossType.description: Whether the stop loss value is a percentage of entry price or an absolute price distance

takeProfit.description: Take profit value as a percentage or absolute price distance

takeProfitType.description: Whether the take profit value is a percentage or an absolute price distance

trailingStop.description: Trailing stop distance as a percentage that follows the price in the profitable direction

maxPositions.description: Maximum number of concurrent open positions allowed

PositionConfig.description: Configuration for trade entry sizing, direction, and order type.

side.description: The allowed trade direction for the strategy

orderType.description: The order type used when entering positions

quantityType.description: The unit for the quantity value

BacktestResult.description: Results from running a backtest on a strategy against historical market data.

strategyId.description: The identifier of the strategy that was backtested

startDate.description: The start date of the backtest period

endDate.description: The end date of the backtest period

initialCapital.description: The starting capital amount in USD

finalCapital.description: The ending capital amount after all trades

totalReturn.description: Total return as a decimal (e.g., 0.15 for 15%)

winningTrades.description: Number of trades that were profitable

losingTrades.description: Number of trades that resulted in a loss

winRate.description: Percentage of winning trades as a decimal between 0 and 1

maxDrawdown.description: Maximum peak-to-trough decline as a decimal

profitFactor.description: Ratio of gross profits to gross losses
